data.narrative:
Horizon Quantum Computing is a public-stage quantum computing company founded by Joe Fitzsimons that has raised $131M+ including a $110M PIPE closed in December 2025. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach across six same-sector, same-stage quantum computing companies: IonQ ($11.0B), Quantinuum ($10.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and one additional peer. This comparable universe spans three public quantum computing firms (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and eight private players.

The peer-median method yielded an implied valuation of $3.8B, representing the midpoint of the six-company cohort. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, ranging from a $60M floor to an $11.0B ceiling, reflecting early-stage valuation volatility and differing technical approaches within quantum computing. Given the limited methodological input available—one triangulation method only—the analysis supports a narrow band rather than a robust range.

The valuation range is $3.2B (low), $3.8B (median), and $4.3B (high), derived from the peer-median anchor. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer cohort given the wide valuation spread, the absence of revenue or profitability metrics in this nascent sector, and technology-risk differentiation across quantum modalities not captured in median pricing. Additional methods such as DCF or revenue multiples would strengthen conviction but are not applicable with available data.
